creator: cejkar@fit.vutbr.cz
text: Ceri Davies wrote (2006/11/02): > > I think that I have found a solution here - the patch below simply > > joins two ... blocks into one, where white space removal > > does not occur in sgml nor in xsl case. > It's wrong though; they are separate span's and should be marked up as > such. Why? I think that ... just wants to say, when to display the text included. And if there should be skipped or included two adjacent texts, why the space between then should be displayed in all cases? (The space is then removed probably too, but I wanted to express logical explanation here, why I do not understand, why there should be two spans and not just one.) -- Rudolf Cejka http://www.fit.vutbr.cz/~cejkar Brno University of Technology, Faculty of Information Technology Bozetechova 2, 612 66 Brno, Czech Republic
